headline: After the PR is merged, GitHub Pages automatically shows version 1.1.0
summary: This example demonstrates the complete GitOps loop: branch, PR, CI, merge, CD, and a visible page update.
learningPath: Create a feature branch from main to prepare release 1.1.0.
Submit a pull request so CI can validate the version and page state.
Complete review in the PR and confirm that all checks pass.
Merge into main so CD rebuilds the site and writes the new commit hash.
Let GitHub Pages publish the update so students can see the new version immediately.
